details.description: Betaxolol is predicted to increase the risk of hypertension and bradycardia when given with Adrenaline/epinephrine. Manufacturer advises caution.
Both Betaxolol and Alcohol can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Alectinib can increase the risk of bradycardia.
Both Betaxolol and Alfentanil can increase the risk of bradycardia.
Both Betaxolol and Aliskiren can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Alprostadil can increase the risk of hypotension.
Betaxolol is predicted to increase the risk of bronchospasm when given with Aminophylline. Manufacturer advises avoid.
Both Betaxolol and Amlodipine can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Apraclonidine can increase the risk of bradycardia.
Both Betaxolol and Apraclonidine can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Aripiprazole can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Asenapine can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Avanafil can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Baclofen can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Bendroflumethiazide can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Benperidol can increase the risk of hypotension.

Both Betaxolol and Crizotinib can increase the risk of bradycardia.
Both Betaxolol and Dapagliflozin can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Desflurane can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Diazoxide can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Digoxin can increase the risk of bradycardia.
Both Betaxolol and Dipyridamole can increase the risk of hypotension.
Disopyramide is predicted to increase the risk of cardiovascular adverse effects when given with Betaxolol. Manufacturer advises use with caution or avoid.
Betaxolol increases the risk of hypertension and bradycardia when given with Dobutamine. Manufacturer advises caution.
Both Betaxolol and Doxazosin can increase the risk of hypotension.
Both Betaxolol and Doxepin can increase the risk of hypotension.

Both Betaxolol and Dronedarone can increase the risk of bradycardia.

Both Betaxolol and Ertugliflozin can increase the risk of hypotension.

Both Betaxolol and Etomidate can increase the risk of hypotension.

Both Betaxolol and Isosorbide mononitrate can increase the risk of hypotension.

Both Betaxolol and Ketamine can increase the risk of hypotension.

Betaxolol is predicted to increase the risk of bradycardia when given with Lanreotide. Manufacturer advises caution.
